muxt generate --use-receiver-type=Server --use-datastar
muxt check

exec go test -race -count=1

-- template.gohtml --
{{- define "GET /script.js Widget(ctx, script)" -}}console.log("{{- .Result -}}");{{- end -}}
-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.25
-- server.go --
package server

import (
	"context"
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type Server struct{}

// Widget renders the same-named template as a text/javascript body. The script
// callback passes the data rendered as .Result in the template.
func (Server) Widget(ctx context.Context, script func(msg string) error) {
	_ = script("hi")
}
-- server_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func TestWidget(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, Server{})

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/script.js", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
		t.Fatalf("status = %d, want %d", rec.Code, http.StatusOK)
	}
	if ct := rec.Header().Get("Content-Type"); ct != "text/javascript" {
		t.Fatalf("content-type = %q, want text/javascript", ct)
	}
	if got := strings.TrimSpace(rec.Body.String()); got != `console.log("hi");` {
		t.Fatalf("body = %q, want %q", got, `console.log("hi");`)
	}
}
